name: Green Tomato and Walnut Relish
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup packed fresh flat-leafed parsley leaves (washed well, spun dry, and chopped)
1 pinch dried hot red pepper flakes, if desired
recipeInstructions:
1. Using a long-handled fork, char the tomatoes over an open flame, turning them until skins are blackened, 2 to 8 minutes.
2. Alternatively, broil the tomatoes on a rack of a broiler pan about 4 inches from the heat, turning them until skins are blistered and charred, 15 to 25 minutes.
3. Transfer the tomatoes to a plate and let stand until cool enough to handle.
4. Peel and core the tomatoes, then chop coarsely.
5. In a bowl, toss the chopped tomatoes with the remaining ingredients and season with salt and pepper.
6. Let the relish stand, covered, at room temperature for 3 hours to develop flavors.
7. The relish may be made up to 2 days ahead and chilled, covered.
8. Serve the relish at room temperature.
Yield: about 3 cups
